name: Hammer Curl
resistance_profile: Neutral grip emphasizes the brachialis and brachioradialis alongside the biceps.
rom_notes: Full extension to full flexion, neutral (thumbs-up) grip.
execution_steps: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms-facing-in, thumbs-up) grip.
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides and your wrists straight.
Curl the dumbbells up while maintaining the neutral grip throughout.
Lower under control to a full extension without swinging the elbows forward.
good_when: You want to build the brachialis and forearm (brachioradialis) alongside the biceps.
A palms-up (supinated) grip bothers your wrists or elbows.
bad_when: You want to maximize the supinated biceps peak (use a supinating dumbbell or barbell curl).
You want a stretch-biased curl (use an incline curl).
progressions: Incline hammer curl for more stretch
